description: [Retention Psychologist] [LLM] feature_value_recap alpha+=4, beta+=-1 — Boosting feature_value_recap should increase routing toward an action that reinforces learner identity and accumulated progress, leveraging sunk cost and habit momentum without relying on heavier incentives that could erode trust.

[Offer Economist] [LLM] discount_cap 1 -> 0 — Non-discount saves already outperform both discount arms while discounts risk unnecessary margin loss, so temporarily capping discounts should shift traffic to the more efficient frontier of pause/downgrade/value actions and improve save_rate without eroding trust.

[Lifecycle Strategist] [LLM] deny targeted_discount_40 for break — Users signaling a temporary break are a lifecycle fit for pause-oriented retention rather than high-discount rescue, so blocking 40% discount there should preserve trust and concentrate traffic on stronger stage-matched save paths like pause_plan_relief and downgrade_lite_switch.

[Support Concierge] [LLM] concierge_recovery alpha+=4, beta+=-1 — Concierge recovery is already a top non-discount performer with limited data, so modestly increasing its prior should route more eligible users to a high-trust save path without overcommitting exploration.

[Trust Guardian] [LLM] deny targeted_discount_40 for quality_bug — Blocking a deep discount for quality_bug prevents a trust-eroding mismatch where users reporting product problems feel bought off, while preserving routing toward more credible recovery actions like concierge_recovery.

[Experiment Operator] [LLM] exploration_rate 0.150 -> 0.080 — With only 300 resolved outcomes and several strong arms already showing materially better win rates than control, reducing exploration from 0.15 to 0.08 should shift more traffic to proven non-discount winners while preserving enough learning to avoid trust-harming overcommitment.

[Product Storyteller] [LLM] feature_value_recap alpha+=3, beta+=-1 — Feature_value_recap is already a strong non-discount performer and best fits a product-storytelling approach, so modestly favoring it should increase saves by helping more users recognize value already created without adding trust risk.

[Deadline Operator] [LLM] exam_sprint_focus alpha+=4, beta+=-1 — For deadline-driven users, a modest prior boost to exam_sprint_focus should increase selection of a trust-preserving urgency-relevant save path that is currently underexplored relative to stronger generic arms.

[Win-Back Researcher] [LLM] context break|free|pause_plan_relief alpha+=3, beta+=-1 — Users signaling a break on the free plan are classic dormant/fatigued cases, and strengthening pause_plan_relief in this already high-performing context should lift saves by favoring a low-pressure comeback path that preserves trust.

[Retention Psychologist] [LLM] pause_plan_relief alpha+=4, beta+=-1 — Boosting pause_plan_relief should improve saves by favoring a high-performing, trust-preserving action that leverages habit continuity and sunk progress for learners who are wavering rather than truly done.

[Offer Economist] [LLM] deny targeted_discount_40 for feature_gap — 40% discount appears overpowered for a non-price objection while strong non-discount arms already outperform globally, so blocking it for feature-gap users should preserve trust and shift traffic to higher-LTV saves without reducing overall save rate.

[Lifecycle Strategist] [LLM] context graduating|free|downgrade_lite_switch alpha+=3, beta+=-1 — Users in a graduating free-plan lifecycle are showing the clearest positive response to a lower-commitment downgrade path, so strengthening this context-action pairing should raise saves without introducing trust risk.
